title: 2001 Dorsey Arbitration Award — Seniority List
content: Arbitration Award under Labour Relations Code Section 84. Arbitrator: James E. Dorsey, Q.C. Date of Decision: June 5, 2001. Between BCTF/Bulkley Valley Teachers' Union (Union) and BCPSEA/Board of School Trustees of School District No. 54 Bulkley Valley (Employer). Re: Seniority List. The union grieved that the employer made several errors in preparing the seniority list of teachers. The union sought specific remedies for individual teachers and a general remedy designed to produce an authoritative list complying with the seniority provisions of the collective agreement. The dispute arose because the employer gave initial notice of layoff on March 14, 2001 to 69 of the 186 teachers with seniority. The union and employer agreed to mediation-arbitration to resolve disputes concerning the staffing process. SENIORITY START DATE (Art. C-7.3.3): The union and employer agreed that the date in the start date column is to be either September 1st or January 1st. If the teacher's start date was the second or subsequent day in the school year in September or January, that second or subsequent date will be the teacher's start date. The appointment date is the earlier of the dates the teacher signified acceptance of employment by signing a contract letter or by actually commencing the assignment. TOC DAYS AS SECOND LEVEL TIEBREAKER (Art. C-7.3.2): As a general rule, seniority does not include time worked as a Teacher-On-Call. The number of TOC days is the second level tiebreaker. TOC days counted since January 1, 1986. Part-time teaching is credited as if it were full-time service for calculating length of service (Art. C-7.2.3). SENIORITY TIEBREAKERS (Article C-7.3): 1st: greatest present continuous employment with the Board (Art. C-7.3.1); 2nd: greatest number of days of substitute teaching with the Board since January 1, 1986 (Art. C-7.3.2); 3rd: earliest date of acceptance of employment with the Board (Art. C-7.3.3); 4th: greatest service recognized for increment purposes (Art. C-7.3.4). The seniority list must be forwarded to the Association by October 15 of each year (Art. C-7.9.1), showing length of seniority as of September 1 of that year. Leave of absence: certain leaves during which seniority was not accumulated are recorded as adjustments on the seniority list.

title: 2004 LOU — Workforce Adjustment Program (Early Retirement Incentive)
content: Letter of Understanding between the Bulkley Valley Teachers' Association, the British Columbia Teachers' Federation, the Board of School Trustees of School District No. 54 (Bulkley Valley), and the British Columbia Public School Employers' Association. Re: Workforce Adjustment Program. Date: May 7, 2004. This LOU expired June 30, 2004 and is of historical significance only. The parties agreed that an early retirement incentive as outlined in the attached plan would be offered to employees in School District No. 54 (Bulkley Valley) covered by the collective agreement. PLAN ELIGIBILITY: The plan was offered to any teacher who: (1) is 55 years old or older as of August 31, 2004; (2) has been employed with School District 54 (Bulkley Valley) for a minimum of 25 years; (3) currently holds a continuing appointment and assignment; (4) is prepared to resign effective June 30, 2004 and retires under the Teachers Pension Plan effective July 1, 2004. PLAN DETAILS: The plan provided payment of a maximum of $5,000 to each eligible applicant with a 1.0 FTE assignment. For those with less than 1.0 FTE, the amount was pro-rated. The Board limited the total amount paid out to $25,000. If more than 5 approved applicants, the $25,000 was divided equally among applicants. Successful applicants were not eligible to work on a continuing or temporary appointment for a minimum of 1 year. Applications were due by 3:00 p.m. Monday, May 10, 2004. This plan was without prejudice and precedent to any other school district and/or any other school year.

title: 2010 Letter — Secondary Preparation Time
content: Letter from Superintendent Beverly J. Young to BVTU President Charlene Watts, dated April 28, 2010, School District No. 54 (Bulkley Valley). Re: Preparation Time - Secondary. Effective September 1, 2010, secondary schools will be organized as follows: (a) Assignments for teachers will be calculated on an F.T.E. (10 month) basis. For example, a teacher teaching 4 blocks in one semester only will be considered as having a .5 teaching assignment. This has ramifications for preparation time because the Collective Agreement states 'Part time teachers teaching 60% or more shall receive prep time pro-rated to their instructional time.' (b) Preparation time for part time secondary employees will be calculated out of 7 blocks. For example, teachers teaching 5 instructional blocks (.63) will have a teaching assignment of .7143 (.71) which includes preparation time. RELATED LOU — PREP TIME PERSONAL LEAVE (signed May 16, 2006, retro to September 1, 2005): Letter of Understanding re Article D.4: Preparation Time / Personal Leave of Absence, offered to full time continuing secondary school teachers. Plan eligibility: (1) full time secondary teacher (1.0 FTE) at date of personal leave application; (2) currently holds a continuing appointment and assignment. Plan details for teacher taking leave in the SECOND semester: if assignment is 4 blocks in first semester, teacher paid out for prep time by end of June in an amount equal to prep time owed for the first semester; if assignment is 3 blocks + 1 block prep in first semester, teacher paid at .844 FTE for the first semester. Plan details for teacher taking leave in the FIRST semester: if assignment is 4 blocks in second semester, teacher paid at 1.0 FTE plus 12.5% for prep time for the second semester; if assignment is 3 blocks + 1 block prep in second semester, teacher paid at .844 FTE for the second semester. Note: One block equals 25% of the teaching day, three blocks equals 75%, four blocks equals 100%. Applications must be received in accordance with Leaves of Absence, Article G.16 (Extended). If a teacher taking leave in the second semester owes money from being overpaid in the first semester, funds must be repaid by end of June. The Board reserves the right to refuse the personal leave request if there are any additional costs to the Board.

title: 2013 LOA — Elementary Preparation Time Scheduling (Article D.4.5)
content: Letter of Agreement — Variance of Collective Agreement Article D.4.5. Between Bulkley Valley School District #54 and Bulkley Valley Teachers' Union. Agreed February 25, 2013. Signed: Karin Bachman (BVTU President) and Chris A. van der Mark (Bulkley Valley SD #54). RE: Resolution Specifics — Grievance 54-2012-05 — Elementary Preparation Time — Collective Agreement Article D.4.5. Article D.4.5 states: all full-time elementary teachers shall receive preparation time equal to 100 minutes per week. Related article D.4.7: Part time teachers teaching 60% or more shall receive prep time pro-rated to their instructional time. TERMS: (1) Effective for the 2013-2014 school year and subsequent years, principals will make every effort to schedule elementary preparation time on Tuesday, Wednesday and Thursday each week. (2) If preparation time for a teacher has to be scheduled on Monday or Friday, the following guidelines apply: (a) A teacher will not have preparation time scheduled on both Monday and Friday in a given school year; (b) A teacher will not have preparation time scheduled on a Monday or Friday for two consecutive school years. (3) If conditions outlined in provision 2a or 2b occur, the principal will arrange for missed prep blocks scheduled on Monday or Friday to be made up within the month the prep block is missed. (4) This agreement may be reviewed at a future date at the request of either party. Note: This agreement was renewed and re-signed on May 14, 2024 (see 2024 D.4.5 Elementary Prep LOA).

title: 2014 Settlement Agreement — TTOC Pay Grievance
content: Grievance Settlement Agreement between British Columbia Public School Employers' Association / Board of Education of School District No. 54 (Bulkley Valley) (Employer) and British Columbia Teachers' Federation / Bulkley Valley Teachers' Association (Union). Dated January 21, 2014 in Smithers BC. Re: TTOC Pay Grievance (Grievances 54.2012.08 and 54.2013.01). The Union filed two grievances alleging violation of the Teachers Teaching on Call pay provisions of the Collective Agreement in relation to secondary school TTOCs called in for less than a full day. Parties engaged in mediation prior to arbitration. SETTLEMENT TERMS: (1) TTOCs will be paid 0.25 of a day's pay per block worked. (2) TTOCs called in to teach a class in the morning (first, second and third blocks) will receive at least two consecutive blocks of work and be paid at least 50% of a day's pay reflecting the amount worked. (3) Where a single block of work occurs in the afternoon (fourth block), the Employer will make all reasonable efforts to also schedule the TTOC in the third block before the lunch break. (4) This settlement agreement shall be in effect until the end of the 2013-2014 school year, at which time the parties shall agree to continue it, modify it, or reinstitute the Grievances. (5) The Employer will provide the Union with details of TTOC callouts and efforts made to schedule TTOCs for two blocks if there is a single block callout. (6) The Employer will provide TTOCs who were paid at less than 25% per block worked from December 1, 2012 to present with retroactive pay to bring the pay to 25% per block worked. (7) This agreement shall not impact the operation of Article B.2.7(c) of the Collective Agreement. (8) Without prejudice. (9) Arbitrator Chris Sullivan retains jurisdiction to resolve any dispute arising out of interpretation and/or implementation of this Settlement Agreement.

title: 2014 LOA — Continuation of TTOC Pay Settlement
content: Letter of Agreement between School District No. 54 (Bulkley Valley) — SD54 and Bulkley Valley Teachers' Union — BVTU. Dated June 27, 2014. RE: Continuation of Settlement Agreement — TTOC Pay Grievance (54-2012-08 and 54-2013-01). With reference to number four (4) of the settlement agreement signed by both parties on January 21, 2014, the parties hereby agree to continue the resolution terms in effect from September 1, 2015 onward. Signed: Karin Bachman (BVTU President) and Chris van der Mark (Superintendent of Schools). This means the TTOC pay settlement terms from January 2014 continue to apply: TTOCs paid 0.25 of a day's pay per block; morning callouts receive at least two blocks and at least 50% of day's pay; afternoon single block situations: employer makes reasonable efforts to also assign 3rd block.

title: 2015 Settlement Agreement — Part-Time Teachers and Pro-D Day Attendance
content: Settlement Agreement between Bulkley Valley School District #54 — SD54 and Bulkley Valley Teachers' Union — BVTU. Dated February 17, 2015. Signed: Ilona Weiss (BVTU) and Chris A. van der Mark (School District #54). RE: Grievance 54-2014-01, Part time Teachers' Attendance at Pro-D days activities. School District 54 and the BVTU met to discuss this grievance. The Parties agree that SD54 and BVTU will apply the relevant collective agreement clauses in F.22 (Professional Development Days) and B.22 (Salary) as follows: (1) Part time teachers whose normal day of work falls on a Professional Development day will attend that day for the amount of time they normally work on that day. (2) Part time teachers who do not normally work on the weekday that a Professional Development Day falls will attend or not attend that day's activities at his or her discretion. (3) If required to attend, the teacher will be paid in accordance with Article B.22.3.e of the collective agreement.

title: 2017-2018 Guidelines — CUPE Emergency Response Replacements for Teacher Absences
content: School District No. 54 (Bulkley Valley) — 2017-2018. Guidelines for managing the use of CUPE Emergency Response Replacements for Teacher Absences. This structure is applied on a without precedent or prejudice basis. A review with the CUPE Executive occurs each year. GUIDELINES: (1) The school administrator and school board office will have an up-to-date record of SEA/EAs in their school that have been identified by the school Principal/Vice-Principal as being interested in the work, suitable to do the work, and in a position that can be temporarily vacated in times of need for emergency teacher replacement. (2) SEA/EAs are eligible for emergency replacement with consideration to their duties. (3) The school administrator will have confirmed that there are no certified or non-certified TOCs available to meet a portion or all of the regular school teacher's replacement needs on that day. (4) The school administrator will select and assign the SEA/EA to be named as emergency replacement for that day. (5) The SEA/EA will be working under the BVTU contract as a non-certified TOC for that day but will retain CUPE pensionable service and benefits for that day. (6) The SEA/EA will be paid for that day at a non-certified TTOC rate as per BVTU collective agreement.

title: 2017 Arbitration Award — TTOC/Temporary Contract Sick Days
content: Arbitration Award under the Labour Relations Code, R.S.B.C. 1996 c. 244. Between British Columbia Public School Employers' Association / Board of Education of School District No. 54 (Bulkley Valley) (Employer) and British Columbia Teachers' Federation / Bulkley Valley Teachers' Union (Union). Arbitrator: Christopher Sullivan. Dates of hearing: November 21-22, 2016, Smithers BC. Published: June 28, 2017. Re: Temporary Contract Grievance — whether TTOCs can carry over sick leave. The Union filed a grievance December 21, 2012 alleging the Employer violated the Collective Agreement by not allowing Teachers Teaching on Call (TTOCs) to carry over sick leave. HOW TEACHERS ARE EMPLOYED: (1) TTOCs — whose employment rights are set out in Article C.27 of the Collective Agreement; are employees within the meaning of the Labour Relations Code. (2) Teachers on temporary contracts — who have not worked for a sufficient length of time to gain a continuing contract; rights set out in Article C.26. A teacher gains a continuing contract upon having worked ten aggregate months in temporary assignments. (3) Teachers on continuing contracts. A TTOC assignment exceeding 20 days is converted to a temporary contract retroactive to the commencement of the TTOC assignment pursuant to Article C.27.1. SICK LEAVE PROVISIONS (as described in the agreed statement of facts): (1) Continuing contract teacher at 1.0 FTE: entitled to 15 days sick leave per year, credited September 1, added to accumulated sick days from prior years. Unused sick days carried forward year to year. (2) Continuing contract teacher at less than 1.0 FTE or in term position: pro-rated proportion of 15 days credited September 1, plus accumulated sick bank. Unused sick days carried forward year to year. (3) Temporary contract teacher: credited pro rata portion of sick leave associated with the FTE equivalent of the position and anticipated length of the temporary assignment. At the END of the temporary contract, the Employer CANCELS any unused sick days credited to the teacher. (4) TTOC whose assignment converts to temporary contract after 20 days: credited with pro rata portion of sick days retroactively to the first day of assignment; these sick days are available to be drawn on; at the END of the contract, the Employer cancels any unused sick days. (5) TTOC or temporary contract teacher whose status converts to a continuing contract teacher pursuant to Article C.26: retains all accrued, unused sick days in their sick bank in their new status as a continuing contract teacher. The grievance focused on the cancellation of TTOCs' unused sick days in points 3 and 4 above. The BCTF funds and administers a Salary Indemnity Plan (SIP) — once a teacher's sick days reach zero balance through illness or injury they may receive partial salary replacement under the SIP.

title: 2018 Preliminary Award — Class Size/Composition Remedy (Jackson Remedy Rollover)
content: Preliminary Award — Remedy General (BCTF Grievance 99-2018-0002). Between British Columbia Public Schools Employers' Association (Employer) and British Columbia Teachers' Federation (Union). Counsel for Employer: Lindsie M. Thomson. Counsel for Union: Steven Rogers. Date of Decision: September 17, 2018. BACKGROUND: The BC Legislature passed legislation in early February 2002 which prohibited bargaining of class size and class composition and removed pre-existing collective agreement language. The BCTF challenged this as unconstitutional. On November 10, 2016, the Supreme Court of Canada (SCC) determined the legislation was unconstitutional (BCTF v. British Columbia 2016 SCC 49) and ordered the collective agreement language be restored immediately. LOU No. 17 required the parties to re-open negotiations regarding the restored collective agreement provisions. In March 2017, BCPSEA, BCTF and the BC Ministry of Education agreed to the terms of a Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) dealing with how the restored language would function. CLASS SIZE AND COMPOSITION — REMEDY FORMULA: Article 24 of the MOA provides for remedies where the School District made best efforts to achieve full compliance but could not. The remedy formula is: V = (180 minutes) x (P) x (S1 + S2), where V = the value of the additional compensation; P = the percentage of a full-time instructional month that the teacher teaches the class; S1 = the highest number of students enrolled in the class during the month minus the maximum class size for that class; S2 = the number of students by which the class exceeds the class composition limits during the month. Remedies are effective October 1, 2017 (or 22 calendar days from start of class). REMEDY OPTIONS (Art. 24.C): Teacher chooses from: (i) Additional preparation time for the affected teacher; (ii) Additional non-enrolling staffing added to the school; (iii) Additional enrolling staffing to co-teach with the affected teacher; (iv) Other remedies that local parties agree would be appropriate. If not practicable during the school year, local parties meet to determine alternative remedy. PRELIMINARY AWARD: The arbitration board has jurisdiction to impose an alternative remedy when local parties cannot agree. Remedy does not expire at end of school year and carries over (Jackson Remedy Rollover).

title: 2023 Settlement Agreement — School Calendar Grievance
content: Settlement Agreement between British Columbia Public School Employers' Association / Board of Education of School District No. 54 (Bulkley Valley) (Employer) and British Columbia Teachers' Federation / Bulkley Valley Teachers' Union (Union). BCTF File No. 54-2023-0001; Local File No. 54-2022-07; CLASS 23-54-000094. Signed November 20, 2023. Signed by: Tanya Davidson (BVTU), Mike McDiarmid (SD54), Stephanie Low (BCPSEA), Clint Johnston (BCTF). BACKGROUND: The Board of Education has the statutory obligation to prepare a school calendar in accordance with the School Act and School Calendar Regulation. Collective Agreement provides: D.28.6 — On or before May 30th of each school year, a School Calendar for the next school year shall be drafted by the Superintendent and the President of the BVTU pursuant to Regulation 8 and forwarded to the Board; F.22.1 — The number of Professional Development days shall be determined by this Agreement. The dates will be determined annually by June 15th for the following year by the Board and the Pro-D Committee. The Union filed a grievance December 15, 2022. Mediation occurred October 24, 2023. SETTLEMENT TERMS: (1) Prior to providing the Board with a draft school calendar, the Superintendent will consult with the BVTU President and the Pro-D Committee concerning its development. A record of that consultation, including any feedback provided by the union, will be forwarded to the Board in advance of the Board preparing a proposed calendar for public consultation. This consultation meets the Board's obligations under Articles D.28.6 and F.22.1. (2) Nothing in this Settlement Agreement limits the right of the BVTU to provide comments to the Board on the proposed school calendar pursuant to s. 5 of the School Calendar Regulation. (3) The consultation takes effect commencing with the school calendar for the 2025-2026 school year (next consultation anticipated in fall of 2024). (4) This Settlement Agreement does not prevent the Union from grieving school calendars established in the future. (5) Without prejudice or precedent. (6) The parties will review this Settlement Agreement should the statutory process for school calendar approval be substantively amended. (7) Arbitrator Cathy Knapp retains jurisdiction. (8) This Settlement Agreement fully and finally resolves the Grievance.

title: 2023 Arbitration Award — Post & Fill Grievance (Continuing to Continuing)
content: Arbitration Award — Post & Fill Grievance. Between British Columbia Public School Employers' Association / Board of Education of School District No. 54 (Bulkley Valley) (Employer) and BC Teachers' Federation / Bulkley Valley Teachers' Union (Union). Arbitrator: Julie Nichols. Counsel for Employer: Keith Mitchell. Counsel for Union: Michael Prokosh. Dates of hearing: April 27, 2021 and October 11 & 13, 2022. Date of Award: May 9, 2023. This case arises from a Grievance dated July 27, 2018, respecting the interpretation of Article E.22.1 of the Collective Agreement. Article E.22 is entitled Posting and Filling Vacant Positions. ARTICLE E.22.1 PROVIDES (in the 2013-2019 Collective Agreement): 'In this Article vacancy means a newly created position or an existing position vacated by the incumbent which the Board intends to fill. All vacancies of 20 days or more shall be posted. All teachers in the District are eligible to apply for all vacancies. During spring post and fill (May to June 30), when a teacher has applied for and accepted an assignment for the coming year, that teacher will not be eligible to apply for further assignments which are posted unless the assignments are continuing or will increase his/her FTE for the following year.' THE DISPUTE: The Union takes the position that teachers can apply for any continuing assignment in subsequent rounds, regardless of whether there is an increase to FTE. The Employer maintains that teachers who accept a continuing position in the first round are not eligible to apply for a position in subsequent rounds unless the position increases their FTE. That is, teachers can only apply for positions in later rounds that improve their situation (i.e., by virtue of being a continuing position or a position of greater FTE). BACKGROUND: Post & Fill Process (P&F) typically involves several rounds of postings from May to June 30. Staffing Process Guidelines (2007 and subsequent years) were developed jointly. A 'job fair' process was used historically. Witnesses included former BVTU Presidents Karin Bachman, Ilona Weiss, and Catherine Quanstrom, and District staff Rod Allen, Bev Young, Toni Perreault, Chris van der Mark, and Bobbie Kingsmill. Prior to 2013, the language of Article E.22.1 had been in place since at least the 1992-1994 Collective Agreement. The 2006-2011 version did not include the phrase 'or will increase his/her FTE for the following year.' This additional language was added in the 2013-2019 Collective Agreement.

title: 2024 LOA — Elementary Preparation Time Scheduling (Renewal of D.4.5 Agreement)
content: Letter of Agreement — Variance of Collective Agreement Article D.4.5. Between Bulkley Valley School District #54 and Bulkley Valley Teachers' Union. Agreed May 14, 2024. Signed: Tanya Davidson (Bulkley Valley Teachers' Union) and Michael McDiarmid (School District #54 (Bulkley Valley)). RE: Resolution Specifics — Grievance 54-2012-05 — Elementary Preparation time — Collective Agreement Article D.4.5. Article D.4.5: All full-time elementary teachers shall receive preparation time equal to the current contractual minutes per week. Related article D.4.7: Part time teachers teaching 60% or more shall receive prep time pro-rated to their instructional time. TERMS: (1) Effective for the 2013-2014 school year and subsequent years, principals will make every effort to schedule elementary preparation time on Tuesday, Wednesday and Thursday each week. (2) If preparation time for a teacher has to be scheduled on Monday or Friday, the following guidelines apply: (a) A teacher will not have preparation time scheduled on both Monday and Friday in a given school year; (b) A teacher will not have preparation time scheduled on a Monday or Friday for two consecutive school years. (3) If conditions outlined in provision 2a or 2b occur, the principal will arrange for missed prep blocks scheduled on Monday or Friday to be made up within the month the prep block is missed. (4) This agreement may be reviewed at a future date at the request of either party. This is a renewal of the original 2013 agreement and continues to be in force.

title: 2024 Settlement Agreement — Labs and Shops Class Size
content: Settlement Agreement between BC Public School Employers' Association / Board of Education of School District No. 54 (Bulkley Valley) (Employer) and BC Teachers' Federation / Bulkley Valley Teachers' Union (Union). Provincial Matters Grievance — BCTF File No. 54-2023-0002; Local File No. 54-2022-06; CLASS File 23-54-000101. Signed: BCPSEA May 3, 2024; BCTF May 7, 2024; SD54 April 30, 2024; BVTU May 6, 2024. Arbitrator: Marguerite Jackson, KC (met April 30, 2024). BACKGROUND: The Union filed the Grievance alleging that the Employer failed to adhere to class size limits for Labs and Shops. The Grievance was referred to arbitration. SETTLEMENT TERMS: (1) The following classes are presently subject to the 'Labs and Shops' class size limit in Article D.1.1: SCIENCE — Science 10, Life Sciences 11, Anatomy and Physiology 12, Chemistry 11 and 12, Physics 11 and 12; SHOPS — Culinary, Woodwork, Metalwork, Automotive Technology (secondary). (2) Science for Citizens 11 is NOT subject to the Labs and Shops class size in Article D.1.1. (3) Should the School District offer a Grade 10, 11 or 12 science class not listed in this Agreement, the parties will discuss whether it ought to be included in the Labs and Shops class size limit. If the parties cannot agree, they can go before Arbitrator Jackson to resolve the dispute. (4) Each year, the Employer will disclose to the Union all Lab and Shop class sizes. (5) Without prejudice or precedent. (6) The Union will withdraw the Grievance after the Lab and Shop class size remedies for the 2022-2023 school year (if any) are resolved. (7) Arbitrator Marguerite Jackson, KC will remain seized with respect to the interpretation and application of this agreement. (8) This agreement is effective upon signing by all parties.

title: Article C.25 — Part Time Teachers' Rights (Current Text & 2025 Proposed Changes)
content: ARTICLE C.25: PART TIME TEACHERS' RIGHTS (current text). C.25.1: A teacher with a continuing full-time appointment to the teaching staff may, without prejudice to that appointment, request a part time appointment for a year or less. If denied, reasons for denial will be provided in writing. C.25.2: At the end of a part time assignment of one year or less, the teacher involved shall be deemed to be in the position he/she vacated for the purposes of the school staff realignment process for the following year unless a further part time appointment is requested by March 1st for the following school year and is granted. C.25.3: Applications for this leave in the following school year must be received no later than April 1. Under special circumstances, an application may be made to the Superintendent (or designate) after April 1. C.25.4: A teacher with a continuing part time appointment may, without prejudice to that appointment, apply for an additional temporary part time appointment for a specified fraction of time or a full time appointment. C.25.5: Teachers on part time continuing appointment, or part time temporary appointment, may request a full time continuing appointment and shall be considered with other applicants pursuant to Article E.22. C.25.6: Two teachers employed full time by the Board may jointly request a job sharing assignment. C.25.7: Job sharing teachers shall: (a) have their salary pro-rated according to the percentage of time worked by each teacher; (b) receive Board benefit contributions as if both teachers were full time teachers; (c) when one of the teachers agrees to work due to the temporary absence or illness of the other teacher, receive payment at full prorated scale placement for all such work. C.25.8: A part time teacher's assignment in elementary schools shall be time tabled such that work periods in any one day are not separated by blocks of time for which the teacher is not paid. C.25.9: Every effort will be made when scheduling part-time teachers in middle and secondary schools prior to the completion of the timetable to avoid assignment in which there are work periods which are separated by blocks of time for which the teacher is not paid. EMPLOYER PROPOSED ARTICLE (January 2025 — Employer Proposal #1): Changes proposed include: (1) Removal of C.25.4 which previously allowed part-time leave for a maximum of (3) school years [struck out by employer]; (2) Renumbering of subsequent articles; (3) New D.24.5 added (Part-time teachers attending staff meetings — when working at multiple schools, consult with principals to determine which school's regular staff meeting to attend). Note: This is the employer's proposal dated January 2025 as part of ongoing bargaining; it has not yet been agreed upon.

title: Article D.22 — Staff Meetings (Current Text & 2025 Proposed Changes)
content: ARTICLE D.22: STAFF MEETINGS (current text). D.22.1: Regularly scheduled staff meetings will be held at each school. An agenda will be provided to all staff three (3) days prior to the meeting. Additional staff meetings may be held: (a) in the case of an emergency; (b) when an issue arises for which the Principal deems an additional meeting is warranted. D.22.2: All staff members shall have the right to place items on the staff meeting agenda. D.22.3: Written minutes of staff meetings shall be kept and circulated to all staff members. D.22.4: Teachers shall not be required to attend staff meetings which commence prior to one (1) hour before classes begin or commence later than one (1) hour after the dismissal of pupils. EMPLOYER'S PROPOSED ARTICLE D.24: STAFF MEETINGS (January 2025 — Employer Proposal #1): D.24.1 [modified]: Regularly scheduled staff meetings will be held at each school. An agenda will be provided to all staff three (3) days prior to the meeting. Additional staff meetings may be held for emergency or when Principal deems warranted. D.24.2 [modified]: All staff members shall have the right to place items on the staff meeting agenda. [Employer adds: 'Staff members may forward items to be considered by the Principal/Vice-Principal for inclusion on the agenda.'] D.24.3: Written minutes of staff meetings shall be kept and circulated to all staff members. D.24.4: Teachers shall not be required to attend staff meetings which commence prior to one (1) hour before classes begin or commence later than one (1) hour after the dismissal of pupils. D.24.5 [NEW — in red, proposed by employer]: Part-time teachers will attend staff meetings. When a teacher works in multiple schools, they will consult with the Principals to determine which school's regular staff meeting they will attend. Note: This is the employer's proposal dated January 2025 as part of ongoing bargaining; it has not yet been agreed upon.

title: 2025-2026 LOU — Thursday Afternoon Early Dismissal
content: Letter of Understanding between Bulkley Valley Teachers' Union and School District 54, Bulkley Valley. Re: Thursday Afternoon Early Dismissal 2025-2026. Dated June 3, 2025. Signed: Tanya Davidson (BVTU) and Michael McDiarmid (SD54). BACKGROUND: The BVTU and the employer both recognize that early dismissal on Thursdays, in effect since approximately 1988, is invaluable to our district in terms of allowing time for: collaboration, parent meetings, lesson planning/preparation, in-service and curriculum implementation. These activities will be taken in consultation with administration as required. THURSDAY AFTERNOON SCHEDULE: First Thursday of each month: school staff meetings may be held. Second Thursday of each month: professional activities as listed in the first paragraph (collaboration, parent meetings, lesson planning, in-service, curriculum implementation). Third Thursday of each month: school based or district meetings — administration will provide staff with the topic or information in a timely manner prior to the meeting. Fourth Thursday of each month: professional activities as listed in the first paragraph. Fifth Thursday (when they occur): professional activities as listed in the first paragraph. During weeks prior to report card submission deadline, every effort will be made to refrain from scheduling events teachers are required to attend. This LOU shall be reviewed by June 2026, at which time this agreement can be extended, amended or ended. This agreement is without prejudice and without precedent.

title: 2025-2026 LOU — Temporary and Small FTE Increases to Existing Contracts
content: Letter of Understanding between Bulkley Valley Teachers' Union and School District 54, Bulkley Valley. Re: Temporary and Small FTE Increases to Existing Contracts 2025-2026. Dated June 3, 2025. Signed: Tanya Davidson (BVTU) and Michael McDiarmid (SD54). Both parties agree that there may be times during the school year that temporary and small FTE contracts may be available at a school. TERMS: Small FTE increases must be: (1) less than 0.2 FTE; (2) temporary; (3) shared with all part-time teachers at that school without the need to post the position; (4) filled based on existing qualifications and seniority language. If no qualified part-time teacher at that school accepts the small FTE increase, then the time will be posted to the district as normal. The BVTU will be notified as soon as possible of any temporary and small FTE increases that occur under this LOU. This LOU shall be reviewed by June 2026, at which time this agreement can be extended, amended or ended. This agreement is without prejudice and without precedent.

title: 2025-2026 LOU — Professional Development as Alternate Remedy Options
content: Letter of Understanding between Bulkley Valley Teachers' Union and School District 54, Bulkley Valley. Re: Professional Development as Alternate Remedy Options 2025-2026. Dated June 3, 2025. Signed: Tanya Davidson (BVTU) and Michael McDiarmid (SD54). BACKGROUND: The provincial Letter of Understanding #12 (RE: Agreement Regarding Restoration of Class Size, Composition, Ratios and Ancillary Language, PART III, Section 16.C) states that once the value of the remedy has been calculated, the teacher will determine which of the following remedies will be awarded: (i) Additional preparation time for the affected teacher; (ii) Additional non-enrolling staffing added to the school specifically to work with the affected teacher's class; (iii) Additional enrolling staffing to co-teach with the affected teacher; (iv) Other remedies that the local parties agree would be appropriate. If not practicable to provide the affected teacher with any of these remedies during the school year, the local parties will meet to determine what alternative remedy the teacher will receive. To facilitate timely access of remedy, administrators will provide teachers with their remedy allocations broken out in minutes and funds as soon as possible. PROFESSIONAL DEVELOPMENT AS ALTERNATE REMEDY (option iv above): The BVTU and the employer recognize the value of Professional Development experiences. REMEDY CONVERTS TO FUNDS FOR PRO-D USE: The teacher with a remedy allocation will discuss their Pro-D intentions with their administrator. All reasonable requests will be approved. Pro-D remedy funds are to support activities, courses or resources that enhance the professional development of the teacher — this can include postsecondary course work. The purchase of technology that directly supports professional development is an acceptable use of this remedy fund. Materials goods (Pro-D resources and/or technology) remain the property of the teacher. When teachers are made aware of their remedy amounts, the District will provide the BVTU a list of teachers and their total remedy. This information will be updated as required. This LOU shall be reviewed by June 2026, at which time this agreement can be extended, amended or ended. This agreement is without prejudice and without precedent.
